items.q: What is the function of dystopian fiction?
items.correct: To critique tendencies in the writer's own society by extrapolating them into a nightmarish future — dystopias hold up a warning mirror to the present.
items.misconception: Dystopian fiction is just storytelling set in scary futures.
items.random_wrong: Dystopian fiction predicts what will actually happen in 100 years.
items.why: Dystopias are about the present, not the future. 1984 critiques 1948; The Handmaid's Tale critiques 1980s America. Students miss the satirical function and read them as prophecy.

items.q: What defines epic poetry as a genre?
items.correct: Long narrative poems on heroic subjects, in elevated style, often invoking a muse, in medias res openings, and treating events of national or cosmic significance.
items.misconception: Epic means any poem that is very long.
items.random_wrong: Epic means any story with a battle in it.
items.why: Length alone isn't enough. Epic has specific conventions (invocation, elevated diction, heroic subject) inherited from Homer through Virgil to Milton — students reduce it to scale.

items.correct: A fatal error or flaw in the tragic hero that leads to their downfall — sometimes a character defect, sometimes a mistake in judgement.
items.misconception: Hamartia is the tragic hero's evil personality.
items.random_wrong: Hamartia is the moment the tragic hero dies.
items.why: Tragic heroes are not villains. Hamartia is often a noble quality taken too far (Othello's love, Macbeth's ambition) or a misjudgement — not simple wickedness.

items.q: The 'kick-off' event of the plot is the.
items.why: It's Inciting incident. “Denouement” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Denouement comes from a French word meaning.
items.why: Correct answer: Untying. “Climbing” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many structure terms listed (exposition, inciting incident, rising action, climax, denouement, prologue)?
items.why: Exact answer: 6. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
